case_title: KENYA LEWIS VS. PETER KARDUM et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER KARDUM, PETER KARDUM AS TRUSTEE, DARLENE PORTILLO MOTION FOR CCP 425.16 SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) CAUSES OF ACTION ONE AND TWO AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES CONTINUED FROM JUL-08-2010 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JUL-19-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES.

REPLY BRIEF IN OPPOS TO PLTF'S OPPOS TO MOTION FOR CCP 425.16 SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) CAUSES OF ACTION ONE AND TWO AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES; SUPPORTING MEMO OF P&A; DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT KARDUM, PETER KARDUM, PETER AS THE TRUSTEE FOR THE PETER KARDUM TRUST

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS PETER KARDUM, PETER KARDUM AS TRUSTEE AND DARLENE PORTILLO'S MOTION FOR CCP 425.16 SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) CAUSES OF ACTION ONE AND TWO AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS MADE A THRESHOLD SHOWING THAT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENCE OF A LEASE INDICATING RENT IS DUE ON THE 15TH OF THE MONTH, DEFENDANT'S RENT LOG WHICH SHOWS DEFENDANT RECEIVED THE AUGUST RENT CHECK, AND A COPY OF THE SEPTEMBER RENT CHECK. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THE MINIMAL MERIT REQUIRED TO DEFEAT THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. SEE YU V. SIGNET BANK/VIRGINIA (2002) 103 CAL. APP. 4TH 298, 317. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT KARDUM, PETER

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Hearing On Motion For Ccp 425.16 Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Causes Of Action One And Two As Alleged In The Complaint For Damages; Supporting Memo Of P&A; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS PETER KARDUM, PETER KARDUM AS TRUSTEE AND DARLENE PORTILLO'S MOTION FOR CCP 425.16 SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) CAUSES OF ACTION ONE AND TWO AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES is denied. Defendant has made a threshold showing that the causes of action arise from protected activity. However, plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claims. Plaintiff presents evidence of a lease indicating rent is due on the 15th of the month, defendant's rent log which shows defendant received the August rent check, and a copy of the September rent check. Plaintiff's evidence demonstrates the minimal merit required to defeat the anti-SLAPP motion. See Yu v. Signet Bank/Virginia (2002) 103 Cal. App. 4th 298, 317. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
